title: assistant-thinking Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-thinking skill's structured reasoning contracts.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-thinking skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against the selected thinking tool methodology and output contract.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Architecture decision selects perspectives
cases.purpose: Checks that an architecture tradeoff uses the perspectives methodology before recommending a path.
cases.prompt: Think through whether we should replace the shell-based skill validator with a C# service. I need a structured recommendation, not just pros and cons.
setup_context: The assistant-thinking skill instructions are active.
The user asks for an architecture decision with real tradeoffs.
No implementation work is requested; the expected output is structured reasoning.
expected_behavior: Selects the perspectives thinking tool for an architecture and design decision.
States the tool selection before analysis and follows the perspectives methodology.
Includes distinct viewpoints such as architect, pragmatist, skeptic, and user advocate or equivalent roles.
Synthesizes convergences and tensions before giving a recommendation.
Includes key insights, a recommendation, confidence, and a genuine dissenting view.
Does not present unsupported free-form pros and cons as the full analysis.
pass_criteria: The response names the selected tool as perspectives.
The response uses perspective-based roles or rounds rather than ad-hoc reasoning.
The recommendation is grounded in the structured analysis.
The confidence level reflects evidence and uncertainty instead of defaulting to high.
The dissenting view is a real counterpoint to the recommendation.
fail_signals: Skips tool selection and writes generic pros and cons.
Gives a recommendation without any methodology.
Claims high confidence without acknowledging tradeoffs or evidence gaps.

cases.title: Risky proposal uses stress test
cases.purpose: Checks that a risky policy proposal is stress-tested with steelman, counter-argument, confidence, and dissent.
cases.prompt: Stress test this proposal: every skill eval must require a live model call before a release can pass.
setup_context: The assistant-thinking skill instructions are active.
The user explicitly asks to stress test a proposal.
The eval itself must remain offline and must not call any provider, model, SDK, or network service.
expected_behavior: Selects the stress test thinking tool and treats the proposal as an argument to evaluate.
Decomposes the proposal into testable claims or equivalent structured elements.
Identifies vulnerabilities from multiple angles such as evidence, edge cases, alternatives, and pragmatism.
Includes a steelman and a counter-argument before the verdict.
Provides a recommendation with confidence and a dissenting view.
Does not perform or require a live model call while evaluating the prompt.
pass_criteria: The response names stress test as the selected tool or method.
The response includes both steelman and counter-argument sections.
The recommendation addresses offline/provider-neutral release constraints.
The confidence level is justified by the analysis depth.
The dissenting view is not suppressed even if the recommendation rejects the proposal.
fail_signals: Calls or requires a provider API to answer the eval.
Accepts the live-call requirement without examining tradeoffs.
Omits recommendation, confidence, or dissenting view.

forbidden_substrings: Live model calls are required to proceed
